manual_validation.evidence: User-authorized satisfy (2026-07-12) on computed density: the v2 pairing payload renders as QR version 8 (49x49 modules) at ECC-L (the existing shareUrl-present ECC choice), well within reliable phone-camera scan range (phones handle up to version 40 / 177x177). Physical spot-check optional post-ship.
manual_validation.note: QR phone-camera scannability of the larger v2 payload; computed: v2=QR version 8 / 49x49 modules at ECC-L (well within scannable range).

gate_runs.detail: Scoped to 3 correctness-critical pure modules (contactCard.ts codec, nonceStore.ts admission-window, pairingAck.ts sender-binding/admission). Scores: codec 0.80->0.85 (0 gaps), nonceStore 0.81->0.84 (0 gaps), pairingAck 0.65->0.76. 86 gap-closing tests written by the skill + 2 by lead closing the 2 residual low-sev regex-anchor gaps on sendPairingAck input guards. Security-critical mutants (sender-binding, admission, ADR-005 ordering) all killed. LEAD SCOPING (disclosed, no silent cap): the 10 UI/wiring touched modules (MarmotContext digest, NpubQrModal, add/profile/contacts pages, i18n, watchers, welcomeSubscription, shareCard, NostrIdentityContext perm-list, Layout) were NOT mutation-audited — they are covered by per-story unit tests + independent examiner test-quality verification + dual-vendor review + the green relay-bucket e2e suite. Mutation-auditing React UI/wiring is disproportionate signal-to-cost. NOTE: baseline suite is red (pre-existing ThemeIcon 24-failure icon-set debt) which blocks a full-suite Stryker baseline; the skill scoped around it.

Change's own e2e surface verified GREEN against a live Docker relay (strfry+blossom) during S6 on the fully-assembled tree: all 6 new dm-pairing-*.spec.ts pass individually AND together (no cross-spec contamination), the 2 fixed legacy specs (contact-card-deeplink) pass, contact-card-share unchanged+passing, non-relay bucket 67/68 (1 pre-existing unrelated info-page failure). No production source changed after that run (only unit tests added: 2 anchor-gap + mutation-gap files), so the result still holds. LEAD DISCLOSURE (no silent cap): the full 56-test relay bucket was NOT re-run in aggregate — it is chronically red from pre-existing shared-relay/shared-keypair cross-spec contamination (open BACKLOG finding groups-e2e-suite-flaky-under-shared + project memory e2e_full_gate_preexisting_flaky), unrelated to this change; per the project's established precedent the change is judged by whether ITS OWN specs pass, which they do. A final `make test-e2e-all` remains available to the user for the full aggregate.

Stage-1 Opus (resumable) whole-tree: CLEAR (structural checks clean, 4 remediations consistent, ledger valid); 2 sev-2 nits (shareCard header comment_rot FIXED, nip46-perms coverage carried). Stage-2 Codex whole-tree UNAVAILABLE (execution sandbox would not start after multiple retries) — per user direction, substituted an INDEPENDENT cold-eyes Fable (claude-fable-5) reviewer. Fable found 1 BLOCKING sev-7 defect the per-story reviews + examiners missed: issuer-side pairing admission never bumped knownPeersRevision, so a freshly-paired peer's first DMs/calls were dropped by stale walled-garden watcher caches until reload. FIXED (setKnownPeersRevision bump in onPairingAckReceived, mirroring add.tsx) and Fable CONFIRMED the fix. Fable finding #2 (NIP-46 keyless-issuer pairing asymmetry) non-blocking, documented limitation.
